questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the ANOVA technique?
options: To calculate the mean of a dataset
To determine the significance of differences between group means
To perform regression analysis
To identify outliers in a dataset
questions.explanation: The ANOVA technique is used to determine the significance of differences between group means in a dataset.

questions.question: What is the main difference between One-way ANOVA and Two-way ANOVA?
options: One-way ANOVA assumes two groups, while Two-way ANOVA assumes three or more groups
One-way ANOVA assumes three or more groups, while Two-way ANOVA assumes two groups
One-way ANOVA is used for continuous data, while Two-way ANOVA is used for categorical data
One-way ANOVA is used for categorical data, while Two-way ANOVA is used for continuous data
questions.explanation: One-way ANOVA is used to compare the means of two or more groups, while Two-way ANOVA is used to compare the means of three or more groups.

questions.question: What is Latin-square Design?
options: A method of arranging data in a square pattern
A method of analyzing data using ANOVA
A method of creating a control group in an experiment
A method of calculating the mean of a dataset
questions.explanation: Latin-square Design is a method of arranging data in a square pattern to control for extraneous variables in an experiment.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using Analysis of Co-Variance (ANCOVA)?
options: To compare the means of two groups with different variances
To compare the means of two groups with different covariates
To control for extraneous variables in an experiment
To identify outliers in a dataset
questions.explanation: The main advantage of using ANCOVA is that it controls for extraneous variables in an experiment by using a covariate.

questions.question: What is the condition for the validity of a Latin-square design?
options: All treatments must be applied once to each level of the factor
All treatments must be applied only once to one level of the factor
All levels of the factor must be applied to all treatments
All treatments must be applied to all levels of the factor
questions.explanation: The condition for the validity of a Latin-square design is that all treatments must be applied once to each level of the factor.
